codeReview.summary: ErrorDisplay component and error classification utilities were created but NOT integrated into the application. The component exists in isolation and is never used by EncodeProgress, CompletionDisplay, or App.tsx. The inline error displays in EncodeProgress (lines 207-212) and CompletionDisplay (lines 144-150) still show hardcoded error messages without using classifyError, getErrorTitle, or getErrorMessage.

issues.description: currentItem.error is passed to EncodeProgress, but EncodeProgress has its own inline error display (hardcoded 'Encoding Failed' title) instead of using the ErrorDisplay component with classification utilities.
EncodeProgress renders an inline error display with hardcoded 'Encoding Failed' title instead of importing and using ErrorDisplay with classifyError/getErrorTitle for user-friendly messages.
CompletionDisplay has its own inline error display with hardcoded 'Encoding Failed' title instead of using ErrorDisplay component with classification utilities.

ErrorDisplay component is created but never imported or used in App.tsx, EncodeProgress.tsx, or CompletionDisplay.tsx. The classifyError, getErrorTitle, getErrorMessage utilities are dead code - never called from any consuming component.
classifyError function includes 'transcode' in its check for encode-failed (line 148) but the word is 'encode' in the test expectations. This works but 'transcode' may never match in practice.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker SKILL.md says for error-handling-component: 'Integrate error states into queue item display'. The worker created the ErrorDisplay component but did not integrate it - they left it as unused standalone code. The skill procedure was not fully followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ErrorDisplay.tsx exists but is never imported in App.tsx, EncodeProgress.tsx, or CompletionDisplay.tsx. Transcript shows worker created ErrorDisplay and tests but did not wire it into existing components.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Duplicate error displays exist across EncodeProgress and CompletionDisplay with nearly identical hardcoded styling instead of using a shared ErrorDisplay component. This violates DRY principle and will cause inconsistent error handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: EncodeProgress.tsx:207-212 has inline error display. CompletionDisplay.tsx:144-150 has inline error display. Both hardcode 'Encoding Failed' as title without classification. ErrorDisplay.tsx utilities (classifyError, getErrorTitle, getErrorMessage) go unused.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill feedback says 'followedProcedure: true' but procedure was not fully executed. The error-handling-component procedure says to 'Integrate error states into queue item display' - integration was not done.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true, but ErrorDisplay is never used in the app. Compare to completion-display-component and encode-progress-component which have inline error handling that predates ErrorDisplay.

summary: The ErrorDisplay component and error classification utilities (classifyError, getErrorTitle, getErrorMessage) were created but NOT integrated into the application. The component exists in isolation while EncodeProgress and CompletionDisplay continue to use their own inline error displays with hardcoded messages. All 170 tests pass, but tests only verify the standalone ErrorDisplay component - they don't verify integration. The feature's expected behavior of 'display error states' and 'show clear error messages' is not fulfilled because the ErrorDisplay is never actually shown to users.
